university_cn_en:
北京工业大学
Beijing University of Technology
evidence_policy_language: 2. Academic Integrity All undergraduate graduation projects (theses) for the class of 2025 are required to undergo academic misconduct detection, which includes comparison against the CNKI (China National Knowledge Infrastructure) database, internal comparisons with theses from the current and previous years within the university, and AI-generated content (AIGC) detection. In principle, the permissible similarity ratio for science and engineering disciplines must not exceed 25%, while for other disciplines it must not exceed 30%. Projects (theses) failing to meet the required similarity ratio will not be eligible to proceed to the graduation project (thesis) defense stage. The direct use of AI-generated content for writing graduation projects (theses) is strictly prohibited. Any such practice, once identified and verified, will be classified as ghostwriting of a graduation project (thesis) and treated as academic misconduct, subject to disciplinary actions in accordance with the university's regulations. If students utilize AI writing tools for auxiliary purposes, it is recommended that they provide a clear statement detailing the usage in relevant sections of their graduation project (thesis), such as the references or acknowledgments.
